title: CTET Paper 2 - Telugu Medium - Test 2
instructions: This test comprises 150 questions, each carrying 1 mark.
All questions are compulsory. There is no negative marking for wrong answers.
The exam duration is 2 hours and 30 minutes (150 minutes).
The timer will start as soon as you click 'Start Test'. The test will auto-submit when the timer reaches 00:00:00.
Use the question palette on the right to navigate directly to any question.
Click 'Save & Next' to save your answer and move to the next question. Answers are NOT saved automatically.
Use 'Mark for Review & Next' to flag a question for later review. If you have answered it, the answer will still be counted.
Use 'Clear Response' to deselect your chosen option for the current question.
You can switch between sections at any time using the section tabs in the header.
Do not refresh or close the browser window during the exam — your progress is saved but it is not recommended.
Click 'Submit' to end the exam before the timer expires. A confirmation screen will appear.

questions.text: Which principle of development states that children gain control of the head before the legs?
questions.explanation: Cephalocaudal development proceeds from head to toe, so head and neck control appear before leg control.

questions.text: In Piaget's theory, conservation of number generally develops during which stage?
questions.explanation: Concrete operational children can conserve number, mass, and volume when they can reason about concrete transformations.

questions.text: Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development refers to the gap between:

options.text: what a learner can do alone and with guidance

questions.explanation: ZPD describes tasks a learner cannot yet do independently but can complete with support from a more knowledgeable person.

questions.text: A teacher giving hints and gradually withdrawing help is using:
questions.explanation: Scaffolding is temporary support that is reduced as the learner becomes more competent.

questions.text: Kohlberg's conventional level of moral development is mainly guided by:
questions.explanation: At the conventional level, children judge actions by social expectations, approval, rules, and authority.

questions.text: Gardner's theory helps teachers understand that intelligence is:
questions.explanation: Multiple intelligences include linguistic, logical, spatial, bodily, musical, interpersonal, intrapersonal, and naturalistic abilities.

questions.text: Erikson's stage most associated with adolescence is:
questions.explanation: Adolescents explore values, roles, and self-concept, making identity formation the key psychosocial task.

questions.text: The strongest example of gender stereotyping in a classroom is:

options.text: asking all students to clean their desks
assigning leadership roles only to boys

questions.explanation: Giving leadership only to boys reinforces a social belief that leadership is male, which is a gender stereotype.

questions.text: A learner with dyscalculia is most likely to need support in:
questions.explanation: Dyscalculia is a specific learning difficulty that affects number sense, calculation, and mathematical reasoning.

questions.text: Universal Design for Learning mainly asks teachers to provide:

options.text: multiple means of engagement, representation, and expression
separate classrooms for children with disabilities

questions.explanation: UDL reduces barriers by offering varied ways to access content, stay engaged, and show learning.

questions.text: In an inclusive classroom, the teacher should first:

options.text: lower expectations for children with disabilities
identify barriers and adapt teaching strategies
ask parents to teach the child at home

questions.explanation: Inclusion keeps high expectations while changing supports, materials, and assessment methods to remove barriers.

questions.text: A child who learns by observing a peer solve a problem illustrates:
questions.explanation: Bandura emphasized observational learning, modelling, imitation, and vicarious reinforcement.

questions.text: In Skinner's operant conditioning, a reward given after a desired response is:
questions.explanation: Positive reinforcement adds a pleasant consequence to increase the likelihood of the behavior.

questions.text: Intrinsic motivation is best shown when a student studies because:
questions.explanation: Intrinsic motivation comes from curiosity, interest, satisfaction, or a sense of competence.

questions.text: Formative assessment is primarily used to:

options.text: rank students at the end of the year

questions.explanation: Formative assessment provides feedback during learning so teaching and learning strategies can be adjusted.

questions.text: A child-centered classroom is characterized by:

options.text: strict lecturing for the whole period

questions.explanation: Child-centered teaching values active exploration, participation, and learning experiences connected to the learner.

questions.text: Assimilation in Piaget's theory means:

options.text: changing an existing schema to fit new information
fitting new information into an existing schema

questions.explanation: Assimilation occurs when a child interprets a new experience using an existing mental structure.

questions.text: Accommodation occurs when a learner:

options.text: changes a schema after new evidence

questions.explanation: Accommodation modifies existing mental structures when current schemas do not explain new experiences.

questions.text: The Right to Education Act 2009 emphasizes education for children aged:
questions.explanation: The RTE Act makes free and compulsory education a right for children from 6 to 14 years of age.

questions.text: A diagnostic test in learning is mainly used to:
questions.explanation: Diagnostic assessment identifies the nature and causes of learning problems so remediation can be planned.

questions.text: A teacher encourages students to give more than one solution to a problem. This promotes:
questions.explanation: Divergent thinking involves generating multiple possible solutions or ideas.

questions.text: In Maslow's hierarchy, which need must be addressed before a hungry child can focus well on learning?
questions.explanation: Physiological needs such as food, water, and rest are basic needs that strongly affect attention and learning.

questions.text: Which is a constructivist teaching practice?

options.text: letting students build concepts through activities

questions.explanation: Constructivism views learners as active makers of meaning through experience, discussion, and reflection.

questions.text: A portfolio is useful because it:

options.text: records only the final exam score
shows growth through collected work over time
compares one child with the whole class

questions.explanation: Portfolios document progress, effort, feedback, and reflection across a period of learning.

questions.text: A child with ADHD may especially require:

options.text: clear routines, short tasks, and movement breaks

questions.explanation: ADHD support often includes structured routines, brief instructions, active tasks, and positive reinforcement.

questions.text: NCF 2005 strongly recommends:

options.text: connecting knowledge to life outside school

questions.explanation: NCF 2005 emphasizes constructivist learning, reducing rote load, and linking school knowledge with life.

questions.text: NEP 2020 highlights which approach for foundational and school learning?
questions.explanation: NEP 2020 values mother tongue, experiential learning, flexibility, skills, and competency-based education.

questions.text: Emotional intelligence includes the ability to:
questions.explanation: Emotional intelligence involves awareness, regulation, empathy, motivation, and social skills.

questions.text: Individual differences mean that teachers should:

options.text: teach every child in exactly one way
use varied strategies and flexible assessment

questions.explanation: Learners differ in readiness, interests, pace, background, and learning preferences, so teaching must be flexible.

questions.text: Peer influence during adolescence is important because adolescents:
questions.explanation: Adolescents often explore identity and social belonging through peer relationships.

questions.text: Read: "The village library opened its doors before sunrise, and children arrived carrying notebooks." What does this suggest?

options.text: The children were eager to learn
The library opened only at night

questions.explanation: Early arrival with notebooks suggests enthusiasm and readiness for learning.

questions.text: In the phrase "a fragile promise", the word fragile means:
questions.explanation: Fragile means delicate or easily damaged, so a fragile promise may not be reliable.

questions.text: The tone of a passage that praises community effort is most likely:
questions.explanation: Praise for shared effort usually creates an appreciative tone.

questions.text: An inference question asks the learner to:
questions.explanation: Inference requires using textual clues and prior knowledge to understand implied meaning.

questions.text: The main idea of a paragraph is:
questions.explanation: The main idea is the central message that supporting details develop.

questions.text: A synonym for "reluctant" is:
questions.explanation: Reluctant means unwilling or hesitant to do something.

questions.text: Which sentence uses the past perfect correctly?

options.text: She has finished before he arrived.
She had finished before he arrived.

questions.explanation: Past perfect describes an action completed before another past action.

questions.text: "The wind whispered through the trees" is an example of:
questions.explanation: Personification gives a human action, whispering, to the wind.

questions.text: A context clue is useful because it helps learners:
questions.explanation: Nearby words and sentences often provide clues to the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

questions.text: A cohesive paragraph should have:
questions.explanation: Cohesion comes from sequence, reference words, connectors, and a clear focus.

questions.text: The communicative approach to language teaching emphasizes:

options.text: meaningful use of language in context

questions.explanation: Communicative Language Teaching focuses on using language for real communication.

questions.text: Krashen's Input Hypothesis suggests that learners benefit from input that is:
questions.explanation: Comprehensible input at i+1 is just beyond the learner's present competence.

questions.text: Language acquisition differs from language learning because acquisition is:
questions.explanation: Acquisition is a natural, meaningful, often subconscious process similar to first-language development.

questions.text: LSRW stands for:
questions.explanation: LSRW represents the four major language skills.

questions.text: A pre-reading activity should mainly:
questions.explanation: Pre-reading tasks prepare learners by connecting topic, vocabulary, and background knowledge.

questions.text: Process writing includes:
questions.explanation: Process writing treats writing as stages of improvement, not only a final product.

questions.text: Functional grammar is best taught by:

options.text: showing how grammar creates meaning in real texts

questions.explanation: Functional grammar connects language forms to purpose and meaning in context.

questions.text: In a Telugu-medium classroom, using Telugu strategically while teaching English can:
questions.explanation: Learners' first language can scaffold understanding and reduce anxiety while English exposure grows.

questions.text: A portfolio in English assessment may include:
questions.explanation: Portfolios collect evidence of language growth across tasks and time.

questions.text: Remedial teaching begins with:
questions.explanation: Remediation should address identified gaps such as decoding, vocabulary, fluency, or grammar use.

questions.text: A learner who reads accurately but too slowly needs support in:
questions.explanation: Fluency involves accuracy, speed, expression, and smooth meaning-making.

questions.text: The Direct Method discourages:

options.text: use of the mother tongue for translation

questions.explanation: The Direct Method emphasizes target-language use and avoids translation.

questions.text: Grammar-Translation Method mainly focuses on:
questions.explanation: Grammar-Translation teaches rules and vocabulary through translation and written exercises.

questions.text: A good speaking activity for language development is:

options.text: role play based on a real situation

questions.explanation: Role play gives learners meaningful oral practice with purpose and audience.

questions.text: Intensive reading involves:

options.text: close reading of a short text for detail

questions.explanation: Intensive reading focuses on detailed comprehension, language features, and careful analysis.

questions.text: Extensive reading helps learners by:

options.text: increasing pleasure reading and vocabulary exposure

questions.explanation: Extensive reading builds fluency, confidence, vocabulary, and reading habit through larger amounts of text.

questions.text: Peer assessment in writing is useful when:

options.text: criteria are clear and respectful feedback is taught

questions.explanation: Peer assessment works when learners use criteria to give constructive feedback.

questions.text: A multilingual classroom should be viewed as:
questions.explanation: Multiple languages can support comparison, meaning-making, identity, and participation.

questions.text: Decoding in reading means:

options.text: connecting letters and sounds to read words

questions.explanation: Decoding is the process of using letter-sound knowledge to recognize written words.

questions.text: The best correction strategy for a speaking activity is to:

options.text: note key errors and give feedback after communication

questions.explanation: During fluency work, delayed feedback protects communication while still supporting improvement.

questions.text: "పాఠశాల తోటలో పిల్లలు మొక్కలు నాటారు. ప్రతి రోజు నీరు పోసి వాటి పెరుగుదలను గమనించారు." ఈ వాక్యాలలో పిల్లల ముఖ్యమైన చర్య ఏమిటి?
questions.explanation: పిల్లలు మొక్కలు నాటడం, నీరు పోయడం, పెరుగుదలను గమనించడం ద్వారా సంరక్షణ చేశారు.

questions.text: "అభివృద్ధి" అనే పదానికి దగ్గర అర్థం ఏది?
questions.explanation: అభివృద్ధి అంటే మెరుగుదల లేదా పురోగతి.

questions.text: ఒక కథలో పాత్ర చేసిన పనుల ఆధారంగా తెలియని భావాన్ని గ్రహించడం ఏ నైపుణ్యం?
questions.explanation: వాక్యాల్లో నేరుగా చెప్పని భావాన్ని ఆధారాలతో గ్రహించడం ఊహాన్వయం.

questions.text: "చెట్టు నీడలో ప్రయాణికుడు విశ్రాంతి తీసుకున్నాడు." ఇందులో చెట్టు పాత్ర ఏమిటి?
questions.explanation: వాక్యంలో చెట్టు నీడ ద్వారా ప్రయాణికుడికి ఉపశమనం కలిగింది.

questions.text: పాఠ్యంలోని ప్రధాన భావం అంటే:
questions.explanation: ప్రధాన భావం పాఠ్యమంతా చెప్పే ముఖ్య ఆలోచన.

questions.text: "ధైర్యంగా" అనే పదానికి వ్యతిరేక పదం ఏది?
questions.explanation: ధైర్యానికి విరుద్ధ భావం భయం.

questions.text: "వాన కురిసింది కాబట్టి నేల తడిసింది." ఈ వాక్యంలో కారణం ఏది?
questions.explanation: వాన కురవడం కారణం; నేల తడవడం ఫలితం.

questions.text: మాతృభాషలో ప్రారంభ విద్య పిల్లలకు ఎందుకు సహాయపడుతుంది?
questions.explanation: మాతృభాష పిల్లల అనుభవాలకు దగ్గరగా ఉండి భావన నిర్మాణాన్ని సులభం చేస్తుంది.

questions.text: తెలుగు బోధనలో LSRW అంటే:
questions.explanation: భాషా అభివృద్ధిలో వినడం, మాట్లాడడం, చదవడం, రాయడం ప్రధాన నైపుణ్యాలు.

questions.text: తెలుగు లిపి నేర్పేటప్పుడు మొదట బలపరచవలసినది:
questions.explanation: అక్షరజ్ఞానం కోసం అచ్చులు, హల్లులు, వాటి ధ్వనులపై పునాది అవసరం.

questions.text: జానపద కథలను తరగతిలో వినియోగించడం వల్ల:
questions.explanation: జానపదం పిల్లల అనుభవాలు, సంస్కృతి, పదసంపదతో భాషను అనుసంధానిస్తుంది.

questions.text: పఠన బోధనలో ముందస్తు చర్చ ఉపయోగం:
questions.explanation: ముందస్తు చర్చ విషయం, పదాలు, అనుభవాలను పాఠంతో కలుపుతుంది.

questions.text: చదవడంలో పదాలను సరిగా పలకలేని పిల్లల కోసం ఉపయుక్తమైన చర్య:
questions.explanation: ధ్వని-అక్షర సంబంధం బలపడితే పద గుర్తింపు మెరుగుపడుతుంది.

questions.text: వ్యాకరణాన్ని సందర్భంలో బోధించడం అంటే:
questions.explanation: సందర్భంలో వ్యాకరణం నేర్పితే రూపం, అర్థం, ఉపయోగం సంబంధం తెలుస్తుంది.

questions.text: పోర్ట్‌ఫోలియో మూల్యాంకనంలో ఉండవచ్చు:
questions.explanation: పోర్ట్‌ఫోలియో కాలక్రమంలో భాషా పురోగతిని చూపుతుంది.

questions.text: సమాసం అంటే:

options.text: రెండు లేదా అంతకంటే ఎక్కువ పదాలు కలసి కొత్త పదం కావడం

questions.explanation: సమాసంలో పదాలు కలసి సంక్షిప్తమైన కొత్త పదరూపం ఏర్పడుతుంది.

questions.text: విభక్తులు ప్రధానంగా సూచించేది:
questions.explanation: విభక్తులు నామవాచకానికి వాక్యంలోని ఇతర పదాలతో ఉన్న సంబంధాన్ని తెలుపుతాయి.

questions.text: మౌఖిక భాషా ప్రవాహం పెంచే చర్య:
questions.explanation: పాత్రధారణ నిజ జీవిత సందర్భంలో మాట్లాడే అవకాశాన్ని ఇస్తుంది.

questions.text: బహుభాషా తరగతిలో ఉపాధ్యాయుని సరైన దృక్పథం:
questions.explanation: పిల్లల భాషలు అర్థనిర్మాణం, భాగస్వామ్యం, కొత్త భాషల నేర్చుకోవడంలో వనరులు.

questions.text: పఠన గ్రహణాన్ని మెరుగుపరచడానికి మంచి వ్యూహం:
questions.explanation: గ్రహణ వ్యూహాలు పాఠ్యంతో చురుకైన సంబంధాన్ని పెంచుతాయి.

questions.text: "సీత పుస్తకం చదివి సారాంశం రాసింది." ఇందులో ముందుగా జరిగిన పని:
questions.explanation: సారాంశం రాయడానికి ముందు పుస్తకం చదవాలి.

questions.text: భాషా అభ్యాసంలో తప్పులను చూడవలసిన విధానం:
questions.explanation: తప్పులు విద్యార్థి ఆలోచనను తెలియజేసి సరైన ప్రతిస్పందనకు దారి చూపుతాయి.

questions.text: తెలుగు పదసంపద పెంచడానికి సహజమైన మార్గం:
questions.explanation: విస్తృత పఠనం కొత్త పదాలను సహజ సందర్భంలో పరిచయం చేస్తుంది.

questions.text: సృజనాత్మక రచనలో ఉపాధ్యాయుడు ప్రోత్సహించవలసినది:
questions.explanation: సృజనాత్మక రచనలో ఊహ, భావవ్యక్తీకరణ, స్వరాన్ని ప్రోత్సహించాలి.

questions.text: చదవడంలో వేగం ఉన్నా అర్థం తక్కువగా ఉంటే దృష్టి పెట్టవలసినది:
questions.explanation: పఠన లక్ష్యం అర్థనిర్మాణం కాబట్టి ప్రశ్నించడం, సారాంశం వంటి వ్యూహాలు అవసరం.

questions.text: ఫార్మేటివ్ మూల్యాంకనం లక్ష్యం:
questions.explanation: ఫార్మేటివ్ మూల్యాంకనం బోధన, అభ్యాసాన్ని సరిచేసేందుకు మధ్యంతర సమాచారం ఇస్తుంది.

questions.text: తెలుగు బోధనలో సమతుల్య పద్ధతి అంటే:

options.text: ధ్వని సాధనతో పాటు సంపూర్ణ పాఠ్య అనుభవం

questions.explanation: సమతుల్య పద్ధతి అక్షర-ధ్వని నైపుణ్యాలు మరియు అర్థవంతమైన పఠనాన్ని కలుపుతుంది.

questions.text: పిల్లల మౌఖిక సంప్రదాయాన్ని ఉపయోగించడం వల్ల:

options.text: మాట్లాడే భాష నుంచి లిఖిత భాషకు వంతెన ఏర్పడుతుంది

questions.explanation: పాటలు, సామెతలు, కథలు మౌఖిక అనుభవాన్ని పఠనం-రచనతో కలుపుతాయి.

questions.text: సరైన ప్రతిస్పందన ఇవ్వడంలో ఉపాధ్యాయుడు:
questions.explanation: నిర్మాణాత్మక ప్రతిస్పందన విద్యార్థికి మెరుగుపడే మార్గాన్ని చూపుతుంది.

questions.text: పాఠ్యంలోని శీర్షిక ఉపయోగం:
questions.explanation: శీర్షిక పాఠ్యంలోని విషయం లేదా కేంద్ర భావంపై పాఠకుడికి సంకేతం ఇస్తుంది.

questions.text: The HCF of 48 and 72 is:
questions.explanation: The common factors include 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, and 24; the greatest is 24.

questions.text: Which number is divisible by 9?
questions.explanation: A number is divisible by 9 if the sum of its digits is divisible by 9; 4+2+1+2=9.

questions.text: The value of (-7) + 12 is:
questions.explanation: Moving 12 units right from -7 on the number line gives 5.

questions.text: The decimal form of 3/8 is:
questions.explanation: Dividing 3 by 8 gives 0.375.

questions.text: Which is an irrational number?
questions.explanation: sqrt(2) cannot be expressed as p/q for integers p and q with q not zero.

questions.text: Using exponent laws, 2^3 x 2^4 equals:
questions.explanation: When bases are the same, exponents are added: 2^(3+4)=2^7.

questions.text: If 5x - 3 = 17, then x equals:
questions.explanation: Add 3 to get 5x=20, then divide by 5 to get x=4.

questions.text: The expansion of (a + b)^2 is:
questions.explanation: Squaring a binomial gives first square, twice the product, and second square.

questions.text: A polynomial of degree 2 is called:
questions.explanation: A polynomial with highest power 2 is a quadratic polynomial.

questions.text: If the discriminant of a quadratic equation is zero, its roots are:
questions.explanation: For ax^2+bx+c=0, D=0 means the two roots coincide.

questions.text: The sum of angles in a triangle is:
questions.explanation: The interior angles of every Euclidean triangle add up to 180 degrees.

questions.text: Which congruence rule uses two sides and the included angle?
questions.explanation: SAS requires two corresponding sides and the included angle to be equal.

questions.text: The distance between (0,0) and (3,4) is:
questions.explanation: By the distance formula, sqrt(3^2+4^2)=5.

questions.text: In a circle, a tangent at any point is perpendicular to the:

options.text: diameter not passing through the point

questions.explanation: The tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

questions.text: sin 30 degrees equals:
questions.explanation: The standard trigonometric value of sin 30 degrees is 1/2.

questions.text: The area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area of a triangle is 1/2 x base x height = 1/2 x 10 x 6 = 30 cm2.

questions.text: The volume of a cuboid of length 5 cm, breadth 4 cm, and height 3 cm is:
questions.explanation: Volume of a cuboid is l x b x h = 5 x 4 x 3 = 60 cm3.

questions.text: Heron's formula finds the area of a triangle when we know:

options.text: only the perimeter of a circle

questions.explanation: Heron's formula uses the semiperimeter and the three side lengths.

questions.text: The mean of 4, 6, 8, and 10 is:
questions.explanation: The sum is 28 and there are 4 values, so mean = 28/4 = 7.

questions.text: The probability of getting a head when a fair coin is tossed once is:
questions.explanation: A fair coin has two equally likely outcomes, so P(head)=1/2.

questions.text: A good mathematics classroom should treat errors as:

options.text: proof that the child cannot learn

questions.explanation: Errors reveal misconceptions and help the teacher plan diagnostic and remedial support.

questions.text: The use of fraction strips mainly supports:
questions.explanation: Manipulatives like fraction strips make part-whole relationships visible.

questions.text: The inductive method in mathematics moves from:
questions.explanation: Inductive teaching helps learners observe patterns and form rules from examples.

questions.text: Diagnostic testing in mathematics helps identify:
questions.explanation: Diagnostic tests locate specific conceptual gaps such as place value, operations, or algebraic reasoning.

questions.text: Ethnomathematics means:

options.text: mathematics in cultural and community practices

questions.explanation: Ethnomathematics connects mathematical ideas to practices in local culture, work, craft, and daily life.

questions.text: A child who writes 1/3 > 1/2 may be overgeneralising:
questions.explanation: This common misconception treats denominators like whole numbers instead of considering part size.

questions.text: Open-ended problems are useful because they:
questions.explanation: Open-ended tasks promote reasoning, communication, creativity, and comparison of methods.

questions.text: In teaching geometry, dynamic software such as GeoGebra helps learners:
questions.explanation: Dynamic geometry tools allow exploration of properties that remain unchanged under movement.

questions.text: Performance-based assessment in mathematics may include:

options.text: explaining a solution with a model

questions.explanation: Performance tasks assess application, reasoning, representation, and communication.

questions.text: Differentiated instruction in mathematics means:

options.text: changing support based on learners' needs
giving no tasks to slow learners

questions.explanation: Differentiation adjusts content, process, product, or support while keeping meaningful learning goals.

questions.text: Which nutrient is mainly needed for body-building and repair?
questions.explanation: Proteins support growth and repair of body tissues.

questions.text: Deficiency of vitamin C causes:
questions.explanation: Scurvy is caused by vitamin C deficiency and may lead to bleeding gums and weakness.

questions.text: The process used to separate grain from chaff by wind is:
questions.explanation: Winnowing separates heavier grains from lighter chaff using moving air.

questions.text: Which fibre is synthetic?
questions.explanation: Nylon is a man-made synthetic fibre, unlike cotton, wool, and jute.

questions.text: Litmus turns red in:
questions.explanation: Blue litmus turns red in acidic solutions.

questions.text: A thermosetting plastic is:
questions.explanation: Bakelite hardens permanently on heating and cannot be remoulded.

questions.text: The atomic number of an element equals the number of:
questions.explanation: Atomic number is defined by the number of protons in the nucleus.

questions.text: Isotopes of an element have:

options.text: same atomic number but different mass number
different atomic number but same mass number

questions.explanation: Isotopes have the same protons but different neutrons, so their mass numbers differ.

questions.text: The functional group in ethanol is:
questions.explanation: Ethanol is an alcohol and contains the hydroxyl group, -OH.

questions.text: The basic structural and functional unit of life is:
questions.explanation: Cells are the smallest living units capable of carrying out life processes.

questions.text: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouse of the cell because they:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP during cellular respiration.

questions.text: Xylem mainly transports:
questions.explanation: Xylem carries water and dissolved minerals from roots to other parts of the plant.

questions.text: Binary fission is a form of:
questions.explanation: In binary fission one parent cell divides into two daughter cells without gametes.

questions.text: In a food chain, producers are usually:
questions.explanation: Green plants produce food by photosynthesis and form the first trophic level.

questions.text: A reflex arc includes:

options.text: receptor, sensory neuron, spinal cord, motor neuron, effector

questions.explanation: A reflex arc is the neural pathway for a quick automatic response.

questions.text: The SI unit of force is:
questions.explanation: Force is measured in newtons in the SI system.

questions.text: Newton's first law is also called the law of:
questions.explanation: The first law states that a body resists change in its state of motion, which is inertia.

questions.text: Ohm's law states:
questions.explanation: Ohm's law relates potential difference, current, and resistance as V=IR.

questions.text: A convex lens is used to correct:
questions.explanation: Hypermetropia is corrected using a converging, or convex, lens.

questions.text: The gas mainly responsible for ozone layer depletion is:
questions.explanation: CFCs release chlorine radicals that destroy ozone molecules in the stratosphere.

questions.text: Inquiry-based science teaching begins with:

options.text: a question or problem to investigate

questions.explanation: Inquiry learning starts from curiosity, questions, observation, evidence, and explanation.

questions.text: A good science experiment should have:

options.text: a fair test with controlled variables

questions.explanation: Controlling variables helps test the effect of one factor reliably.

questions.text: Concept cartoons are useful because they:
questions.explanation: Concept cartoons present alternative ideas and help students debate evidence and misconceptions.

questions.text: A common misconception about plants is that they:
questions.explanation: Plants respire all the time, though photosynthesis occurs only in light.

questions.text: Laboratory work should be assessed by:

options.text: observation of skills, safety, records, and reasoning

questions.explanation: Practical assessment includes process skills, safe handling, data recording, and interpretation.

questions.text: A model of the human heart is useful because it:

options.text: makes an invisible internal structure easier to visualise

questions.explanation: Models help students visualise structures and relationships, though their limits should be discussed.

questions.text: Environmental education in science should promote:
questions.explanation: Science education connects environmental concepts with responsible decisions and sustainable practices.

questions.text: An exit ticket is an example of:
questions.explanation: Exit tickets quickly reveal what students understood at the end of a lesson.

questions.text: Teaching weight and mass should clarify that:

options.text: mass and weight are identical everywhere
mass is amount of matter, weight is gravitational force
mass changes with location like weight

questions.explanation: Mass measures matter, while weight depends on gravitational force acting on that mass.

questions.text: STEM integration encourages students to:

options.text: connect science, technology, engineering, and mathematics in problem solving

questions.explanation: STEM tasks integrate concepts and practices across disciplines to solve real problems.
